findings.description: Cover page layout is simplified and the large centered title block is not preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a centered cover with wide margins, title, subtitle, logo, and release date placed low on the page inside a border. HTML page 1 presents the same content in a standard top-down document flow with the title aligned near the top and reduced whitespace.

findings.description: The HTML omits the PDF's explicit continuation markers for multi-page tables, so some section/page structure is lost.
findings.evidence: PDF includes repeated headings such as "Number of Coastal Recreational Fishing Licenses sold by North Carolina county of sale ... (Continued)", "... county of residence ... (Continued)", "... state of residence ... (Continued)", and "... country of residence ... (Continued)" on subsequent pages. HTML consolidates each into a single section/table without the "(Continued)" headings.

findings.description: At least one table cell value is incorrect in the HTML in the Marine Resources Fund Balance table.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, under Marine Resources Fund Balance, row "Transaction Fee" and column "FY2014" shows "$ 500,094". The HTML has "$ 500,094"? Actually the PDF visually appears to show "$ 500,094" while total is "$ 5,543,636"; this row should be checked carefully. More clearly, in the same table the PDF row label appears as "Sales Commission" under Gross Revenue, but the HTML includes it as a monetary row inside the main revenue table while preserving values. However, the strongest discrepancy visible is formatting/structure in project rows where note text belongs in a separate notes column in PDF, but HTML merges it into project names, e.g., PDF has "DMF NCE-Inshore & Oyster Video" with note "Roll over amount from FY 2009-13" aligned separately; HTML renders "DMF NCE-Inshore & Oyster Video Roll over amount from FY 2009-13" in one cell.

findings.description: The 'Projects Approved for Funding from the Marine Resources Fund' table loses the PDF's separate note/annotation column and merges notes into project titles.
findings.evidence: PDF shows project names in one column and rollover notes such as "Roll over amount from FY 2009-13" and "Roll over amount from FY 2013" placed separately. HTML merges them into single project cells: "DMF NCE-Inshore & Oyster Video Roll over amount from FY 2009-13" and "WRC Boating Access Projects on hold/NCE Roll over amount from FY 2013".

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is inaccurate: the HTML uses two h1 elements for the title/subtitle and does not reflect section headings as headings.
findings.evidence:
HTML begins with two top-level headings: "

Coastal Recreational Fishing License

" and "

Sales Update

". Major section titles from the PDF such as "Marine Resources Fund Balance", "Marine Resources Endowment Fund Balance", "Projects Approved for Funding from the Marine Resources Fund...", and "SUMMARY" are rendered as bold paragraphs instead of heading elements.
